engineGuide.h2: BMW M4 Engine Replacement - All Variants Covered

entries.title: 3.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The S55 debuted with the F82/F83 M4, marking the M division’s definitive move from high-revving naturally aspirated V8s to force-fed straight-six efficiency without sacrificing power.
commonFailures: Crank hub spin / failure (critical risk, especially on tuned or high-rev manual cars)
entries.cta: Get quotes for S55B30 BMW M4 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The ultimate evolution of the S55, reserved for the limited-run M4 GTS. It introduced water injection technology to cool the intake charge and produce a factory-rated 493 hp.
commonFailures: Water injection system complexity / pump failure (GTS)
Crank hub failure (less common than base S55, but higher stakes)
DME software glitches causing vanos faults
entries.cta: Get quotes for S55B30T0 BMW M4 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The S58 replaced the S55 with a closed-deck block, forged internals, and a pair of mono-scroll turbos. It immediately set a new benchmark for BMW M straight-six performance.
commonFailures: Oil pump / oil pressure regulation issues (early G8x cars)
Injector seal leaks / high-pressure fuel pump failure
Crank hub vulnerability (carried over from S55 on manual cars)
entries.cta: Get quotes for S58B30 BMW M4 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: A subtle calibration variant of the base S58, primarily distinguished by software tuning adjustments for specific market model years.
commonFailures: Similar to S58B30 (oil pump, injector seals)
Software-related drivetrain warning lights (addressable via ISTA update)
entries.cta: Get quotes for S58B30O0 BMW M4 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The flagship S58, tuned to 550 hp for the lightweight, track-focused M4 CSL. It features unique engine management mapping and enhanced cooling.
commonFailures: Oil starvation under sustained high-G cornering
entries.cta: Get quotes for S58B30T0 BMW M4 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: BMW M4 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The S55-powered F82/F83 M4 (2014-2020) and S58-powered G82/G83 M4 (2021-present) are among the most capable straight-six turbo engines ever built, but they are not immune to failure. Crank hub issues on the S55 and oil pump concerns on the S58 dominate UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Below, we break down the most common failure modes, what they cost to fix at a dealer versus a specialist, and when replacement makes more sense than repair.

problems.h4: S55 Crank Hub Failure - The Most Searched M4 Engine Problem
problems.affectedModels: BMW M4 Coupe (F82), Convertible (F83), Competition (2014-2020, S55B30 all variants including manual and DCT)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 30,000 - 70,000 miles (often triggered by sudden load changes or aggressive downshifts)
problems.rootCause: The crank hub is secured by a friction disc rather than a conventional keyway. Under high torque spikes or sudden drivetrain shock, the hub can spin relative to the crankshaft, throwing off cam timing and often causing piston-to-valve contact.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Crank Hub Upgrade - Prevention before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit aftermarket pinned or keyed crank hub conversion
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix if caught early; no further crank hub risk

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Catastrophic failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt S55 with upgraded crank hub, rod bearings, timing chain assembly
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month warranty; upgraded components address original failure points

problems.recommendation: If your M4 is still running but you have noticed drivetrain malfunctions or timing faults, a preventative crank hub upgrade is the most cost-effective route at £3,500-£5,500 from a specialist. If the hub has already spun and caused valve or piston damage, replacement is almost always cheaper than attempting a cylinder head rebuild. On an M4 worth £18,000-£35,000, a quality rebuilt engine with an upgraded hub restores reliability and resale value.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt S55B30 engine prices for your BMW M4

problems.h4: S55 Rod Bearing Wear - The Track-Day Owner’s Nightmare
problems.affectedModels: BMW M4 Competition, CS, high-mileage Coupe/Convertible (2014-2020, S55B30, particularly cars used on track or with frequent cold starts)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 50,000 - 90,000 miles (accelerated by poor warm-up habits and extended oil change intervals)
problems.rootCause: The S55 uses tight-clearance bearings that are sensitive to oil viscosity and warm-up procedures. Prolonged periods of cold oil or extended service intervals lead to bearing material fatigue, eventually causing knocking and potential rod failure.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Bearing Replacement - Caught early (no knocking)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace rod bearings and bolts with upgraded aftermarket units
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000+ miles if done before damage; good preventative value on high-mileage cars

repairOptions.tier: Major - Crankshaft Grind & Oversized Bearings - Knocking but salvageable
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove crank, grind journals, fit oversized bearings and new shells
repairOptions.longevity: Viable for light knock; engine must be stripped completely

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Spun bearing damaged crank/block
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt S55 with new crank, bearings, oil pump
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable outcome once bearing failure has progressed

problems.recommendation: If you hear a knocking sound from the bottom end, stop driving immediately. A specialist can drop the sump and inspect bearings before deciding. Caught early with no crank damage, bearing replacement is a no-brainer at £1,800-£2,800. Once the crank is scored or a rod has failed, replacement is the only safe long-term solution. For track-used M4s, we recommend preventative bearing replacement every 50,000-60,000 miles.
problems.cta: Get quotes for S55 rod bearing replacement and rebuilt engines

problems.h4: S58 Oil Pump Failure - The G82 Generation’s Weak Link
problems.affectedModels: BMW M4 Coupe (G82), Convertible (G83), Competition, Competition xDrive (2021-2024, early production S58B30 engines)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 15,000 - 40,000 miles (oil pressure warning lights appearing under load or cornering)
problems.rootCause: The oil pump pressure regulation valve can stick or fail, leading to a sudden drop in oil pressure. High-G cornering in xDrive models may also expose oil pick-up issues.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Oil Pump Replacement - No bottom end damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace oil pump assembly, oil pan gasket, fresh oil and filter
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix if bearings are still intact

repairOptions.tier: Major - Oil Pump + Bearings - Brief pressure loss caught early
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace oil pump, rod bearings, main bolts, oil flush
repairOptions.longevity: Adds safety margin; recommended if pressure dropped below 1 bar

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Starvation caused bearing/crank failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt S58 with revised oil pump and upgraded bearings
repairOptions.longevity: Only option once engine has seized or knocked

problems.recommendation: If your G82 M4 flags an oil pressure warning, stop immediately and tow to a specialist. A simple oil pump replacement at £2,200-£3,200 saves the engine. Driving even a short distance with low pressure almost always leads to bearing and crank damage, pushing you into replacement territory. For early 2021-2022 cars, consider a preventative oil pump upgrade.
problems.cta: Compare S58B30 rebuilt engine prices for BMW M4 G82/G83

problems.h4: S55 Turbocharger Failure - Boost Loss and White Smoke
problems.affectedModels: BMW M4 Coupe, Convertible, Competition, CS (2014-2020, S55B30, typically one or both turbos failing)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000 - 100,000 miles (oil seals degrading, shaft play developing)
problems.rootCause: The S55’s twin turbos run hot and are oil-cooled. Over time, carbon build-up and degraded oil seals cause shaft play, leading to boost leaks, oil consumption, and in severe cases, turbo overspeed.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Turbo Rebuild - One turbo, early shaft play
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, strip, clean, replace bearings and seals on one turbo
repairOptions.longevity: Good for 40,000-60,000 miles; cost-effective if other turbo healthy

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Both Turbos Replaced / Rebuilt - Both units worn
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace or rebuild both turbos, new gaskets, oil and coolant lines
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if one turbo failed; the other often follows

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with New Turbos - Turbo debris entered engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt S55 with new turbos, oil cooler, full flush
repairOptions.longevity: Required if a turbo has disintegrated and sent metal through the engine

problems.recommendation: White smoke from the exhaust or a persistent loss of boost points to turbo seals. A specialist turbo rebuild at £900-£1,500 per unit is excellent value if caught early. However, if the car has been driven for months with a failed turbo, metal contamination may have reached the oil system. In that case, a full rebuilt engine with new turbos is the only safe route. We recommend changing oil at 5,000-mile intervals on tuned S55 cars to protect turbo bearings.
problems.cta: Compare S55 turbo replacement and rebuilt engine quotes

problems.h4: S58 Charge Cooler Failure - Coolant Loss and Power Reduction
problems.affectedModels: BMW M4 G82, G83, Competition (2021-present, S58B30, air-to-water charge coolers mounted in the V of the engine)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 20,000 - 50,000 miles (coolant level dropping without external leaks)
problems.rootCause: The plastic charge cooler housing can crack or the internal seals can fail, allowing coolant to be drawn into the intake manifold and burned in the combustion chambers.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Charge Cooler Replacement - No engine damage yet
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace charge cooler, pressure test cooling system, fresh coolant
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix if caught before coolant hydro-locks engine

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Charge Cooler + Plugs & Coils - Misfires from coolant ingestion
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace charge cooler, all spark plugs, ignition coils, oil change
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses misfire damage caused by coolant contamination

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Hydro-lock or bearing damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt S58 with new charge cooler, bottom end inspection
repairOptions.longevity: Required if coolant has bent a rod or washed bearings

problems.recommendation: If your G82 M4 is consuming coolant but has no visible external leak, suspect the charge cooler. A replacement at £1,400-£2,200 from a specialist is straightforward. Ignoring low coolant warnings can lead to hydrolock, where coolant fills a cylinder on startup and bends a connecting rod. At that point, replacement is inevitable. Check coolant levels monthly on S58 cars.
problems.cta: Get quotes for S58 charge cooler replacement and rebuilt engines

faq.h2: BMW M4 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a BMW M4 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: £9,500-£16,500 for a rebuilt S55 (F82/F83) and £14,500-£24,000 for a rebuilt S58 (G82/G83) supply-only. Specialist prices are typically 30-40% below main dealer quotes for the same work.
keyPoints: S55 rebuilt engines (2014-2020): £9,500-£14,000 from specialist suppliers
S58 rebuilt engines (2021-present): £14,500-£22,000 supply-only
Add £850-£1,500 for professional supply & fit installation
Main dealer replacement typically £17,000-£25,000 for S55 and £20,000-£30,000 for S58
items.cta: Compare BMW M4 engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is the BMW M4 S55 engine crank hub failure really that serious?
items.answer: Yes - it's the Achilles' heel of the S55. When the crank hub spins, it throws engine timing off, often causing pistons to hit valves. Prevention costs £2,000-£3,500; full engine replacement costs £9,500+.
keyPoints: Crank hub failure can destroy the engine with no warning signs
Tuned cars face significantly higher risk than stock vehicles
Keyed or pinned hub upgrade permanently eliminates the failure risk
Replacement S55 engines from specialists include upgraded hubs as standard
items.cta: Get quotes for upgraded S55 crank hub engine replacements

items.question: Are the G82 M4 S58 engines more reliable than the old S55?
items.answer: Yes - the S58 uses a closed-deck block and forged internals that handle far more abuse. BMW drift cars run 1,000hp through stock S58 crankshafts with minimal wear after years of racing .
keyPoints: Closed-deck crankcase eliminates cylinder wall flex under high boost
Forged crankshaft and connecting rods from factory
Crank hub design is more secure than S55 (fewer reported failures)
350-bar direct injection and 3D-printed cylinder head core
items.cta: Compare rebuilt S58 engine prices for your G82 M4

items.question: What are the most common problems on the BMW M4 S55 engine?
items.answer: Crank hub failure dominates replacement enquiries, followed by rod bearing wear (50k-90k miles) and turbocharger oil seal failure around 54k-70k miles. Each can destroy the engine if ignored.
keyPoints: Crank hub slip - most searched M4 engine problem in the UK
Rod bearing wear - track-driven cars at highest risk
Turbo failure - white smoke from exhaust indicates oil seal failure
Replacement turbos cost £1,300-£2,000 each from specialists vs £7,000-£8,000 from BMW

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my BMW M4 or should I scrap it?
items.answer: Replace it. An M4 with a fresh rebuilt engine is worth £18,000-£35,000 depending on year and variant. A quality rebuilt S55 costs £9,500-£14,000 - far less than the £20,000+ depreciation on a replacement car.
keyPoints: F82 M4 values remain strong - replacement makes financial sense
Rebuilt engines include upgraded components (crank hub, bearings) to prevent repeat failure
Specialist rebuilds come with 12-24 month warranty for peace of mind
Scrapping a salvageable M4 loses the car's residual value completely
items.cta: Get a quote for BMW M4 engine replacement vs repair assessment

items.question: How long do BMW M4 engines last before needing replacement?
items.answer: Well-maintained S55 engines typically reach 100k-150k miles. S58 engines are newer but expected to exceed this. Crank hub failure can strike at any mileage - it's mileage-independent and driven by driving style.
keyPoints: Regular oil changes every 5k-6k miles extend S55 bearing life
Track use and tuning significantly reduce engine lifespan
Crank hub risk doesn't increase with mileage - it's unpredictable
Preventative hub upgrade is the only guaranteed protection
items.cta: Find a rebuilt BMW M4 engine with enhanced components

items.question: Can I upgrade my BMW M4 engine when getting it replaced?
items.answer: Yes - many specialists offer upgraded internals during replacement. Stage 1 builds with forged rods and upgraded turbos are common. Stock S58 internals handle 1,000hp without modification .
keyPoints: Upgraded crank hub (keyed/pinned) eliminates S55's main weakness
Forged pistons and rods available for high-power builds
S58 closed-deck block supports 800+hp on stock internals
Aftermarket turbos and charge coolers available during replacement
items.cta: Enquire about performance-upgraded BMW M4 replacement engines

items.question: What's the difference between rebuilt, reconditioned and used M4 engines?
items.answer: Rebuilt engines are stripped and reassembled with new wear parts (typically £9,500-£14,000). Reconditioned means cleaned and tested with selective part replacement. Used engines are as-removed from donor cars (£4,500-£8,000).
keyPoints: Rebuilt includes new bearings, seals, rings - often upgraded components
Recon engines stop at inspection and replacement of failed parts only
Used engines carry original factory weaknesses (crank hub, bearings)
All EngineMarket rebuilt units include 12-month unlimited warranty
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs reconditioned BMW M4 engine prices

items.question: Can I get a BMW M4 engine with supply and fit included?
items.answer: Yes - most specialist suppliers offer fitting through their garage networks. Budget £850-£1,500 for professional installation including ISN coding, immobiliser synchronisation and full system testing.
keyPoints: Supply & fit removes logistics and workshop sourcing hassle
Includes all gaskets, fluids, filters and labour for installation
ISN coding ensures ECU and immobiliser accept the replacement engine
Single warranty point for both engine and labour
items.cta: Find BMW M4 engine supply & fit deals near you

items.question: How do I know which engine code my BMW M4 has?
items.answer: Check the engine block stamp (S55B30 for F82/F83, S58B30 for G82/G83), look at your V5C logbook, or enter your registration above for instant identification. Competition models use the same base codes but different ECU mapping.
keyPoints: S55B30: F82/F83 M4 Coupe/Convertible 2014-2020 (425-444hp)
S55B30T0: M4 GTS (493hp) and CS (454hp)
S58B30T0: M4 CSL (550hp) and CS (543hp)
items.cta: Use our registration checker to identify your BMW M4 engine code instantly
